qa_matrix.question_text: Member Name
Member Date of Birth
ID Proof Aadhar
Education Qualiﬁcation
Your current Occupation
Kindly share your current location( City and state with country)?

You Vakada Pavani , declar e that, you have fully understood the questions ask ed to you as per this call and have furnished complete, true and accurate infor mation af ter fully understanding the same. Sir/Mam' ar e you in agr eement with the declaration that I have r ead out to you.
qa_matrix.captured_response: Okay, madam. All the answers you've given are true and will be included in this policy. Thank you for the information and cooperation, madam. Have a nice day.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Read the full declaration as per MER.

qa_matrix.question_text: In the last 24 months, have you smoked or used any tobacco or nicotine products?
qa_matrix.captured_response: I haven't done anything like that.

qa_matrix.question_text: Do you consume alcohol ?
Have you ever been advised by a doctor to reduce or stop alcohol consumption ?
Have you ever used recreational drugs or Narcotics that was not prescribed by a Licensed physician ?
What is Proposed insured height (cm)?
What is Proposed Insured weight (kg)?
qa_matrix.captured_response: 70 kgs, but before I was 60-68 or so.

qa_matrix.question_text: High/Low blood pressure, raised cholesterol or triglycerides or lipids

Chest pain or heaviness or discomfort, heart murmur, heart attack, coronary artery disease, Heart valve disorder, congenital heart disease, palpitation, rheumatic fever, shortness of breath or any other heart conditions
Cancer or carcinoma-in-situ, pre-cancerous condition, leukaemia, enlarged nodes, unusual skin lesions, dysplasia, leukoplakia, sacroma, tumours, polyps, nodules, cysts, lump or other growths
Diabetes, raised blood sugar or sugar in urine,Hyperthyroidism, Hypothyroidism , other thyroid disorders, or any other endocrine disorders
Asthma, bronchitis, sinusitis, tuberculosis, pneumonia, chronic or recurrent cough, sleep apnea, emphysema, chronic pulmonary disease (COPD) or any other respiratory disorders
Hepatitis, hepatitis carrier, fatty liver, liver cirrhosis, jaundice, alcoholic liver disease or any other diseases or disorders of the liver
Anemia, thalassemia, systemic lupus erythematosus or any other diseases or disorders of the blood or autoimmune disease

Anxiety, depression, epilepsy, paralysis, weakness of limb, stroke, transient ischemic attack (TIA), multiple sclerosis, Parkinson's disease, Dementia, Alzheimer's or any other neurological, nervous, mental health disorders?

Gastritis, gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), stomach ulcer, recurrent indigestion, irritable bowel syndrome, Barrett's esophagus, atrophic gastritis, Crohn's disease, ulcera-tive colitis, Pancreatitis/Pancreatic disorder or any gastrointestinal diseases or disorders

Kidney stones, gall stones, kidney or urinary tract infection, blood or protein in urine, chronic kidney disease, kidney failure, piles, fistula, hydrocele or any other disorders of the kidney, bladder, genital, prostate or urinary systems?
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Ask about piles, fistula, hydrocele, and prostate disorders.

qa_matrix.question_text: Recurrent Back pain, arthritis, gout, slipped disc, muscular dystrophies, deformity, physical disability or any other disorders of muscle, bones, joints, limbs or spine?
Blindness,cataracts, glaucoma, impaired sight, double vision or any other diseases or disorders of eyes
Deafness, Impaired hearing, speech problem, hoarseness of voice, difficult in swal-lowing, ear discharge or any other diseases or disorders of ears, nose or throat

Is there anything else that you would like to share with us with respect to your health/ habits or anything else that you believe is important to ensure that policy is processed basis all facts & information?

Any abnormal medical findings or pending test results, scheduled or planned or pre-scribed medical tests such as imaging scan reports (e.g. x-ray, ultrasound, MRI, PET scan, CT scan etc.), biopsy, electrocardiogram (ECG), treadmill, Mammography, Endo-scopy/Colonoscopy, PAP Smear, blood or urine test
Medication or treatment that lasted more than 2 weeks or had been referred to a specialist doctor or hospital for any treatment/consultation and/or investigations (eg: Blood, ECG, MRI, Xray etc)
Inability to perform your daily activities or absent from work more than two 2 consecutive weeks at a time
Have you suffered from any congenital defect or disorder or hereditary disease such as cerebral palsy, Down's syndrome, poliomyelitis
Have you or your spouse or partner been told to undergo tests or treatment, or received any medical advice or counselling in connection with sexually transmitted diseases, including HIV and AIDS?
qa_matrix.captured_response: We did it for the pregnancy test recently. Ah, normal, normal, yes.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Yes, underwent HIV tests for pregnancy, reports normal.

qa_matrix.question_text: Apart from what you have disclosed, are you experiencing any unexplained weight loss/gain or any sign/symptoms that you are likely to seek medical advice or treatment for or awaiting any surgical procedure.
qa_matrix.captured_response: Because of pregnancy, I gained some weight, that's the only difference. Nothing.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Yes, weight gain due to pregnancy.

qa_matrix.question_text: Are you Pregnant now?
How many weeks pregnant are you?
Have you ever suffered/are currently suffering from gestational diabetes, preeclampsia or any other pregnancy related complications?

Have you ever suffered from or consulted a doctor for breast lump(s), breast disease, irregular or painful menstruation, abnormal Pap Smear or any other breasts or gyneco-logical conditions such as vaginal disorders, or disorder of the uterus, ovary, fallopian tubes ?

critical_issues: The full legal declaration was not read to the customer.
The doctor recorded 'No' for medication lasting more than 2 weeks, despite the customer stating she is taking medicine for pregnancy.
The doctor recorded 'No' for HIV/AIDS tests, despite the customer stating she underwent tests for pregnancy (which were normal).
The doctor recorded 'No' for unexplained weight loss/gain, despite the customer stating weight gain due to pregnancy.
The customer's stated education ('Degree') differs from the MER ('Above Graduate').
The customer's stated current location ('Nandigama, Krishna district') differs significantly from the MER ('SRIKAKULAM, ANDHRA PRADESH, INDIA').
The doctor did not explicitly ask about all specific conditions listed in the MER for kidney/urinary disorders (piles, fistula, hydrocele, prostate).
The doctor did not explicitly ask about 'Impaired hearing' as part of ear/nose/throat disorders.
recommendations: The doctor must read the complete legal declaration verbatim to the customer at the end of the call.

Ensure all health-related information, including ongoing medications, tests, and conditions like pregnancy-related weight changes, is accurately captured and reflected in the MER, even if considered normal.
Verify and accurately record all personal particulars, such as education qualification and current location, ensuring consistency between the conversation and the MER.
For questions that list multiple conditions, the doctor should ensure all specific conditions are explicitly mentioned or covered during the questioning to avoid incomplete information capture.

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer is cooperative and answers questions.
Customer confirms understanding of language preference.

validation_errors: Location: Transcript 'Nandigama, Krishna district' vs MER 'SRIKAKULAM, ANDHRA PRADESH, INDIA'
Medication for >2 weeks: Customer states taking medicine for pregnancy, MER states 'No'.
HIV/AIDS tests: Customer states undergoing tests for pregnancy, MER states 'No'.
Weight gain: Customer states weight gain due to pregnancy, MER states 'No' for unexplained weight loss/gain.

examples: Oh. Hmm, that's somewhere else. One minute, please. One minute, please stay on the line, will you wait?

greetings.explanation: The doctor initiated greetings at the beginning of both calls.

call_opening.explanation: The doctor introduced herself, stated the company name, and confirmed the client's name at the start of the call.

language_preference.explanation: The doctor asked for the customer's language preference early in the call.

id_validation.explanation: The doctor requested the customer's Aadhaar or PAN card number for identification.

disclaimer.explanation: The doctor clearly stated that the call would be recorded and that the answers would form a legal contract.

politeness.explanation: The doctor consistently used polite language, addressing the customer as 'madam' and offering to wait when needed.

empathy.explanation: The customer mentioned pregnancy, which the doctor acknowledged and followed up with relevant medical questions. No serious health concerns requiring specific empathy were present.

communication_skills.explanation: The doctor effectively clarified questions and rephrased when needed, but also repeated some questions unnecessarily, indicating slight redundancy.

probing.explanation: The doctor effectively probed for more specific details when the customer's answers were vague or required further clarification, especially regarding health conditions and pregnancy.

observations.explanation: There were no instances of customer hesitation (refusal, no answer, or repeated evasion) or third-party prompting observed in the transcript.

call_closure.explanation: The doctor provided a clear declaration about the truthfulness of the answers and thanked the customer for their cooperation.
